name: problem-solve
description: Structured problem-solving skill. Create, update, and track problems through a 5-step decision framework.

framework.description: Every problem item uses a structured JSON content format with 5 fields. This forces clear thinking and tracks decisions over time.

fields.problem: What is the problem? (clear, specific statement)
fields.why: Why does this problem exist? (root cause analysis)
fields.action: Chosen action — which solution did you pick and why?
fields.outcome: What happened? (filled in after executing the action)

create_problem.description: Create a new problem entry with the structured template

content.description: JSON string with problem template fields

schema.action: string — chosen action (can be empty initially)

update_problem.description: Update a problem — usually to add action or outcome

content.description: Updated JSON string with problem template fields

body.title: Context switching friction
body.content: {"problem":"Switching between life modes loses momentum","why":"No clear boundaries or transitions between contexts","solutions":"1. Mode-specific views\n2. Ritual prompts\n3. Focus timers","action":"Build mode-specific views first","outcome":""}

{"problem":"Switching between life modes loses momentum","why":"No clear boundaries or transitions between contexts","solutions":"1. Mode-specific views\n2. Ritual prompts\n3. Focus timers","action":"Build mode-specific views first","outcome":"Mode views reduced context-switch time by ~40%"}

workflow: 1. Agent identifies a problem from the user's input
2. Agent creates a problem item: POST /api/items with type='problem' and content as JSON
3. Agent helps the user fill in: problem, why, solutions
4. Agent helps decide on an action
5. Later, the agent or user updates the outcome field via PUT /api/items/:id
